You see, even though it's an uncertain world, we're certain of some things. We're certain that even though the 'evil empire' may have passed, evil still remains. We're certain there are people that can't stand what America stands for. ... We're certain there are madmen in this world, and there's terror, and there's missiles and I'm certain of this, too: I'm certain to maintain the peace, we better have a military of high morale, and I'm certain that under this administration, morale in the military is dangerously low.

quote: I've got a reason for running. I talk about a larger goal, which is to call upon the best of America. It's part of the renewal. It's reform and renewal. Part of the renewal is a set of high standards and to remind people that the greatness of America really does depend on neighbors helping neighbors and children finding mentors. I worry. I'm very worried about, you know, the kid who just wonders whether America is meant for him. I really worry about that. And uh, so, I'm running for a reason. I'm answering this question here and the answer is, you cannot lead America to a positive tomorrow with revenge on one's mind. Revenge is so incredibly negative. And so to answer your question, I'm going to win because people sense my heart, know my sense of optimism and know where I want to lead the country. And I tease people by saying, 'A leader, you can't say, follow me the world is going to be worse.' I'm an optimistic person. I'm an inherently content person. I've got a great sense of where I want to lead and I'm comfortable with why I'm running. And, you know, the call on that speech was, beware. This is going to be a tough campaign.

quote: A lot of times in the rhetoric, people forget the facts. And the facts are that thousands of small businesses Hispanically owned or otherwise pay taxes at the highest marginal rate.
source: To the Hispanic Chamber of Commerce; Washington, D.C., March 19, 2001

quote: I'm also honored to be here with the speaker of the House just happens to be from the state of Illinois. I'd like to describe the speaker as a trustworthy man. He's the kind of fellow who says when he gives you his word he means it. Sometimes that doesn't happen all the time in the political process.
source: Chicago, March 6, 2001 (Thanks to Gary Belkin.)

quote: Of all states that understands local control of schools, Iowa is such a state.
source: Council Bluffs, Iowa, Feb. 28, 2001 (Thanks to Peter Sagal)

quote: I have said that the sanction regime is like Swiss cheese that meant that they weren't very effective.
source: White House press conference, Washington, D.C., Feb. 22, 2001

quote: One reason I like to highlight reading is, reading is the beginnings of the ability to be a good student. And if you can't read, it's going to be hard to realize dreams; it's going to be hard to go to college. So when your teachers say, read you ought to listen to her.
source: Nalle Elementary School, Washington, D.C., Feb 9, 2001

quote: We're concerned about AIDS inside our White House make no mistake about it.
source: Washington, D.C., Feb. 7, 2001

quote: I confirmed to the prime minister that we appreciate our friendship.
source: After meeting with Prime Minister Jean Chr tien of Canada, Feb. 5, 2001

quote: Then I went for a run with the other dog and just walked. And I started thinking about a lot of things. I was able to I can't remember what it was. Oh, the inaugural speech, started thinking through that.
source: Pre-inaugural interview with U.S. News & World Report, Jan. 22, 2001 issue

quote: I'm hopeful. I know there is a lot of ambition in Washington, obviously. But I hope the ambitious realize that they are more likely to succeed with success as opposed to failure.
source: Interview with the Associated Press, Jan. 18, 2001 (Thanks to M. Bateman.)

quote: I want it to be said that the Bush administration was a results-oriented administration, because I believe the results of focusing our attention and energy on teaching children to read and having an education system that's responsive to the child and to the parents, as opposed to mired in a system that refuses to change, will make America what we want it to be a literate country and a hopefuller country.
source: Washington, D.C., Jan. 11, 2001

quote: I do remain confident in Linda. She'll make a fine labor secretary. From what I've read in the press accounts, she's perfectly qualified.
source: Austin, Texas, Jan. 8, 2001

quote: Natural gas is hemispheric. I like to call it hemispheric in nature because it is a product that we can find in our neighborhoods.
source: Austin, Texas, Dec. 20, 2000

quote: The great thing about America is everybody should vote.
source: Austin, Texas, Dec. 8, 2000

quote: As far as the legal hassling and wrangling and posturing in Florida, I would suggest you talk to our team in Florida led by Jim Baker.
source: Crawford, Texas, Nov. 30, 2000

quote: They misunderestimated me.
source: Bentonville, Ark., Nov. 6, 2000

quote: I regret that a private comment I made to the vice presidential candidate made it through the public airways.
source: Allentown, Pa., Sept. 5, 2000.

quote: Anyway, after we go out and work our hearts out, after you go out and help us turn out the vote, after we've convinced the good Americans to vote, and while they're at it, pull that old George W. lever, if I'm the one, when I put my hand on the Bible, when I put my hand on the Bible, that day when they swear us in, when I put my hand on the Bible, I will swear to not to uphold the laws of the land.
source: Toledo, Ohio, Oct. 27, 2000

quote: Families is where our nation finds hope, where wings take dream.
source: LaCrosse, Wis., Oct. 18, 2000

quote: It's important for us to explain to our nation that life is important. It's not only life of babies, but it's life of children living in, you know, the dark dungeons of the Internet.
source: Arlington Heights, Ill., Oct. 24, 2000

quote: It's one thing about insurance, that's a Washington term. Our priorities is our faith.
source: Greensboro, N.C., Oct. 10, 2000

quote: I mean, there needs to be a wholesale effort against racial profiling, which is illiterate children.
source: Second presidential debate, Oct. 11, 2000 (Thanks to Leonard Williams.)

quote: I know the human being and fish can coexist peacefully.
source: Saginaw, Mich., Sept. 29, 2000

quote: It is clear our nation is reliant upon big foreign oil. More and more of our imports come from overseas.
source: Beaverton, Ore., Sep. 25, 2000

quote: Listen, Al Gore is a very tough opponent. He is the incumbent. He represents the incumbency. And a challenger is somebody who generally comes from the pack and wins, if you're going to win. And that's where I'm coming from.
source: Detroit, Sept. 7, 2000 (Thanks to Michael Butler, Houston, Texas.)

quote: The woman who knew that I had dyslexia I never interviewed her.
source: Orange, Calif., Sept. 15, 2000

quote: I don't think we need to be subliminable about the differences between our views on prescription drugs.
source: Orlando, Fla., Sept. 12, 2000

quote: We'll let our friends be the peacekeepers and the great country called America will be the pacemakers.
source: Houston, Texas, Sept. 6, 2000

quote: Well, I think if you say you're going to do something and don't do it, that's trustworthiness.
source: Ibid.

quote: We cannot let terrorists and rogue nations hold this nation hostile or hold our allies hostile.
source: Ibid.

quote: I want you to know that farmers are not going to be secondary thoughts to a Bush administration. They will be in the forethought of our thinking.
source: Salinas, Calif., Aug. 10, 2000 (Thanks to Kris Sester.)

quote: States should have the right to enact reasonable laws and restrictions particularly to end the inhumane practice of ending a life that otherwise could live.
source: Cleveland, June 29, 2000 (Thanks to Douglas Basford.)

quote: The only things that I can tell you is that every case I have reviewed I have been comfortable with the innocence or guilt of the person that I've looked at. I do not believe we've put a guilty ... I mean innocent person to death in the state of Texas.
source: All Things Considered, NPR, June 16, 2000 (Thanks to Andy Nouraee.)

quote: There's not going to be enough people in the system to take advantage of people like me.
source: On the coming Social Security crisis; Wilton, Conn.; June 9, 2000 (Thanks to Andy Mais.)

quote: GOV. BUSH: Because the picture on the newspaper. It just seems so un-American to me, the picture of the guy storming the house with a scared little boy there. I talked to my little brother, Jeb I haven't told this to many people. But he's the governor of I shouldn't call him my little brother--my brother, Jeb, the great governor of Texas
GOV. BUSH: Florida. The state of the Florida.
source: The NewsHour With Jim Lehrer, April 27, 2000

quote: He has certainly earned a reputation as a fantastic mayor, because the results speak for themselves. I mean, New York's a safer place for him to be.
source: On Rudy Giuliani, The Edge With Paula Zahn, May 18, 2000 (Thanks to Peter Goldman.)

quote: You subscribe politics to it. I subscribe freedom to it.
source: Responding to a question about whether he and Al Gore were making the Eli n Gonz lez case a political issue. In Palm Beach, Fla., as quoted by the Associated Press, April 6, 2000 (Thanks to Helen Kennedy.)

quote: It's clearly a budget. It's got a lot of numbers in it.
source: Reuters, May 5, 2000 (Thanks to Allison Fansler.)

quote: Reading is the basics for all learning.
source: Announcing his Reading First initiative in Reston, Va., March 28, 2000 (Thanks to Carl LaRocca.)

quote: It's evolutionary, going from governor to president, and this is a significant step, to be able to vote for yourself on the ballot, and I'll be able to do so next fall, I hope.
source: In an interview with the Associated Press, March 8, 2000 (Thanks to Joshua Micah Marshall.)

quote: I understand small business growth. I was one.
source: New York Daily News, Feb. 19, 2000

quote: Really proud of it. A great campaign. And I'm really pleased with the organization and the thousands of South Carolinians that worked on my behalf. And I'm very gracious and humbled.
source: To Cokie Roberts, This Week, Feb. 20, 2000

quote: How do you know if you don't measure if you have a system that simply suckles kids through?
source: Explaining the need for educational accountability in Beaufort, S.C., Feb. 16, 2000

quote: I've changed my style somewhat, as you know. I'm less I pontificate less, although it may be hard to tell it from this show. And I'm more interacting with people.
source: ibid

quote: This is Preservation Month. I appreciate preservation. It's what you do when you run for president. You gotta preserve.
source: Speaking during Perseverance Monthat Fairgrounds Elementary School in Nashua, N.H. As quoted in the Los Angeles Times, Jan. 28, 2000

quote: I know how hard it is for you to put food on your family.
source: Greater Nashua, N.H., Chamber of Commerce, Jan. 27, 2000

quote: The administration I'll bring is a group of men and women who are focused on what's best for America, honest men and women, decent men and women, women who will see service to our country as a great privilege and who will not stain the house.
source: Des Moines Register debate, Iowa, Jan. 15, 2000

quote: We must all hear the universal call to like your neighbor just like you like to be liked yourself.
source: ibid.

quote: I read the newspaper.
source: In answer to a question about his reading habits, New Hampshire Republican Debate, Dec. 2, 1999

quote: The important question is, How many hands have I shaked?
source: Answering a question about why he hasn't spent more time in New Hampshire, in the New York Times, Oct. 23, 1999

quote: Keep good relations with the Grecians.
source: Quoted in the Economist, June 12, 1999

quote: It was just inebriating what Midland was all about then.
source: From a 1994 interview, as quoted in First Son, by Bill Minutaglio
